Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL E. ADAMS, TEL. NO. (650) 599-9463 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRET R. LANDESS OF OIUM REYEN & PRYOR, TEL. NO. 392-8300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - The court denies defendant's CCP 170.6 challenge as untimely. The court's register of actions (docket) indicates that defendant filed a CCP 170.6 challenge to Judge Goldsmith on August 18, 2014. The court has yet to receive a courtesy copy of the challenge. The challenge appears to be untimely under the 10 day/5 day rule. See CCP 170.6(a)(2) ["If the judge, other than a judge assigned to the case for all purposes, court commissioner, or referee assigned to, or who is scheduled to try, the cause or hear the matter is known at least 10 days before the date set for trial or hearing, the motion shall be made at least 5 days before that date."]. In People v. Sup. Crt. (1993) 4 Cal.4th 1164, 1180-1184, the court explains that where a judge regularly sits in a department and his identity is known or discoverable upon reasonable inquiry at least 10 days before the hearing, the challenge must be made at least 5 days before the hearing date. In the instant matter, Judge Goldsmith regularly sits in the law and motion department and it appears that the challenge is one day late. See CCP 12c(a) [counting days]. The matter was argued. DEFENDANT G. AVERY, M.D.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is taken under submission. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
